title: Core types, Language struct, and parse table lookup
description:
Define all foundational data types and implement parse table lookup. This is the foundation everything else builds on.

Deliverables:
- types.go: Symbol, StateID, FieldID, Point, Range, Length, InputEdit
- language.go: Language struct with all table fields (parseTable, smallParseTable, smallParseTableMap, parseActions, lexModes, symbolMetadata, fieldMapSlices, fieldMapEntries, aliasSequences, reserved words, supertypes). Implement lookup(), tableEntry(), nextState().
- subtree.go: SubtreeHeapData struct with ALL fields (symbol, parseState, padding, size, childCount, visibleChildCount, namedChildCount, visibleDescendantCount, firstLeaf, repeatDepth, productionID, flags including fragileLeft/fragileRight, errorCost, dynamicPrecedence, externalScannerState, structuralHash for AA-2). SubtreeArena for batch allocation ([]SubtreeHeapData backing arrays). Subtree as compact 8-byte value type using uint64: high bit discriminates inline (leaf data packed into remaining bits) vs arena reference (block index + offset). SubtreeID for node identity (arena block + offset pair, no unsafe.Pointer).
- ParseActionEntry as Go struct (Type, Count, Reusable, ShiftState, ShiftExtra, ShiftRepetition, ReduceSymbol, ReduceChildCount, ReduceDynPrec, ReduceProdID).
- Hand-compiled JSON grammar as testdata/json_language.go to validate the representation.

Tests:
- Parse table lookup correctness (both large and small state paths)
- Symbol metadata access
- Subtree leaf creation and accessor correctness
- SubtreeArena allocation and capacity growth
- Inline vs heap subtree discrimination (8-byte Subtree type)
- SubtreeID equality for node identity
- JSON grammar table smoke tests

Reference: docs/design.md §1-3 (note: Subtree is 8 bytes using uint64, see §2 Memory Management), C source: language.c, subtree.c, subtree.h
